topic: Sharp non-equivalence/strict-extension theorem between three leading shift-share / formula IV estimators: canonical Bartik-style shift-share IV, recentered instruments, and design-based formula IV under shock independence plus exposure additivity.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the necessary-and-sufficient frontier at which canonical shock-level SSIV moments, recentered formula-IV moments, and covariate-conditional design-based moments identify the same beta_exp rather than distinct limit functionals?

source_refs.claim: BHJ show that SSIV orthogonality can be represented as shock-level orthogonality and derive consistency from sufficient many-shock, weak-dependence, dispersed-exposure conditions, not an iff frontier among later estimator variants.
source_refs.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull, Xavier Jaravel

source_refs.claim: Borusyak and Hull recenter formula treatments or instruments by subtracting the counterfactual average induced by nonrandom exposure to exogenous shocks.
source_refs.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull

source_refs.claim: The formula-IV review compares design-based formula-instrument tools and notes open inference extensions beyond the currently tractable special cases.
source_refs.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull, Xavier Jaravel

source_refs.excerpt: no axis-b non-equivalence threshold derived in closed form
the strongest open point is the primitive open-class separation

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published shift-share papers establish estimator-specific sufficient conditions: shock-level orthogonality for canonical SSIV, counterfactual-shock recentering for nonrandom exposure, and design-based formula-IV moments. The prior ThmSmith LP/SVAR non-equivalence run shows the adjacent methodological risk: a named-estimator separation collapses unless it is expressed as a primitive quantitative boundary. The missing object is therefore not another sufficient validity condition, but a single iff characterization over the joint geometry of shock dependence, exposure-share covariance, and covariate leakage.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient identification theorem giving equality of the three population moments exactly on an explicit Omega-Pi-epsilon boundary and a strict-separation counterexample immediately outside it.

open_problems.open_problem: Which shock-correlation and exposure-share covariance regimes make correlated shocks harmless for identification, as opposed to merely requiring robust inference or changing the SSIV estimand?

source_refs.claim: AKM show conventional shift-share inference can overreject because residuals are correlated across regions with similar exposure shares and derive inference valid under arbitrary cross-regional residual correlation.
source_refs.author: Rodrigo Adão, Michal Kolesár, Eduardo Morales

source_refs.claim: BHJ's consistency arguments rely on many independent or appropriately clustered shocks with sufficiently dispersed exposure, leaving no sharp dependence-region classification.
source_refs.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull, Xavier Jaravel

source_refs.excerpt: sharply separates the point-identified, partially-identified, and non-identified regimes
the derived flagship-tier spectral phase-transition claim did not deliver
row-space and singular-subspace boundaries in panel event-study regressions

open_problems.why_unsolved: The web literature separates two problems that the proposed frontier would have to unify: AKM treat share-induced correlation as an inference problem, while BHJ impose independence or clustered weak-dependence conditions for consistency. The prior spectral phase-transition record supplies a useful row-space threshold template but also records that a generic spectral slogan did not deliver; the unresolved obstacle is a necessary-and-sufficient operator condition connecting Omega Pi to identification, not only to standard-error validity.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A dependence-space classification theorem mapping Omega and Pi into equivalence, inference-only distortion, and strict identifying-moment distortion regions, with sharp finite-shock witnesses at the boundary.

open_problems.open_problem: When does covariate-conditional shock-mean leakage epsilon get fully absorbed by recentering or design conditioning, and when does it produce a limit functional different from canonical Bartik SSIV?

source_refs.claim: The recentered-instrument approach handles nonrandom exposure by adjusting for the average treatment or instrument over counterfactual shock realizations.
source_refs.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull

source_refs.claim: Formula-IV identification conditions on observed formula inputs and subtracts design expectations, but the review does not classify leakage directions relative to canonical shock-level aggregation.
source_refs.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull, Xavier Jaravel

source_refs.claim: GPSS give a competing exposure-share interpretation of Bartik instruments, highlighting that share-exogeneity and shock-exogeneity routes are distinct.
source_refs.author: Paul Goldsmith-Pinkham, Isaac Sorkin, Henry Swift

source_refs.excerpt: state the observable diagnostic in finite-support matrix form
DML/LRO/ADML population variance maps coincide at the truth

open_problems.why_unsolved: Recentered and formula-IV papers give a way to subtract design expectations, while GPSS shows that exposure-share and shock-level identification logics can diverge. The prior DML run is a warning that orthogonalization-style separations can disappear at the true population functional unless the leakage channel is explicitly pinned down. The unresolved obstacle is to classify the directions of epsilon that lie in the recentering/design-conditioning null space versus directions that survive exposure aggregation through Pi.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An iff projection theorem for epsilon onto the design sigma-field and the column space of Pi, giving equality when the leakage projection vanishes and a strict finite-support witness when it has positive spectral mass.

open_problems.open_problem: Can unrestricted or high-dimensional controls be residualized in shift-share/formula-IV designs without moving the estimator across an equivalence versus non-equivalence boundary?

source_refs.claim: BHJ derive shift-share controls that isolate conditionally random shock variation, but their exposure-robust standard errors rely on structured controls rather than arbitrary high-dimensional residualization.
source_refs.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull, Xavier Jaravel

source_refs.claim: The formula-IV review lists asymptotic inference and extensions outside the clean linear formula/control cases as open methodological directions.
source_refs.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull, Xavier Jaravel

open_problems.why_unsolved: Controls alter the effective exposure covariance Pi and can also change the residual shock-mean leakage epsilon. Existing formula-IV and SSIV treatments provide structured control recipes, but do not give a control-invariant map showing when partialling-out preserves the same population moment and when it creates a different estimand.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A residualization theorem describing how arbitrary controls transform Omega Pi and epsilon, together with valid variance formulas for the transformed design and a counterexample showing strict moment movement.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the efficiency frontier among canonical SSIV, recentered IV, and design-based formula IV under correlated shocks and exposure-share covariance?

source_refs.claim: Borusyak and Hull characterize recentered instruments built from counterfactual shocks and discuss efficient construction within their formula-instrument framework.
source_refs.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull

source_refs.claim: The formula-IV review describes optimal recentered instrument construction and notes that covariance weighting is difficult or infeasible in non-iid settings.
source_refs.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull, Xavier Jaravel

open_problems.why_unsolved: Efficiency and equivalence depend on the same covariance geometry, but current optimal-instrument results are not specialized into a three-way comparison with canonical shock-level SSIV under arbitrary Omega and Pi. The missing obstacle is a covariance-weighted tangent-space comparison that accounts simultaneously for first-stage strength, exposure concentration, and non-iid residual covariance.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A semiparametric efficiency-bound theorem and dominance classification showing when recentered/design-based formula IV strictly improves on, equals, or loses to canonical SSIV as a function of Omega, Pi, first-stage strength, and residual covariance.

literature_map: Track B scanned the shift-share and formula-IV econometrics cluster rather than SCM/do-calculus axiomatics. Borusyak-Hull-Jaravel 2022 ReStud is the canonical shock-level SSIV anchor: SSIV orthogonality maps to shock-level orthogonality under sufficient many-shock conditions. Adão-Kolesár-Morales 2019 QJE is the leading inference counterpoint: exposure shares induce cross-region residual correlation, and robust inference need not settle identification. Goldsmith-Pinkham-Sorkin-Swift 2020 AER supplies the competing share-exogeneity/Rotemberg-weight interpretation. Borusyak-Hull 2023 Econometrica introduces recentered instruments for nonrandom exposure to exogenous shocks. Borusyak-Hull-Jaravel 2025 Econometrics Journal reviews design-based formula instruments and highlights inference/extension limits. The active tension is that validity is understood estimator-by-estimator, while the literature does not give a single sharp geometry for equality versus strict non-equivalence across Omega, Pi, and epsilon. Secondary stressors are unrestricted controls, non-iid covariance weighting, and dynamic or serially correlated shocks.
prior_proposal_map: Track A found no prior ThmSmith proposal explicitly targeting Borusyak-Hull-Jaravel shift-share, Borusyak-Hull recentering, or Borusyak-Hull-Jaravel formula IV. The nearest prior substrates were eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_v1 for named-estimator non-equivalence via a common latent-law/completion boundary; q1_spectral_phase_transition_p1_markov for spectral row-space threshold language; eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1 for finite-support covariance/projection diagnostics and a warning that apparent strict separations can collapse at the truth; pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2 for memory-splitting strict-gain logic; and pid_continuous_iv_no_monotonicity_v1 plus pid_dose_response_iv_v1 for global pasting of local IV margins. The highest-leverage reviewer signal was eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1_reviews/angle2_v3.json strengthening_paths[1], which requested an observable finite-support matrix diagnostic.
